description: Harden cron-runtime singleton lock acquisition with atomic pidfile creation and race coverage.

userStories.title: Acquire the cron singleton lock atomically
userStories.description: As the harness operator, I want cron runtime lock acquisition to be atomic so watchdog/restart races cannot spawn duplicate schedulers.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/cron-runtime.ts is edited only for lock-acquisition behavior and is not deleted, moved, or deprecated.
acquireLock(pidFile) attempts exclusive pidfile creation before stale-lock reclamation.
Live foreign PID locks return false and leave the pidfile unchanged; current-process PID locks return true.
Stale or unparsable pidfiles are reclaimed by unlinking and retrying exclusive creation, re-inspecting if another contender wins before retry.
Vitest coverage proves live, stale, self, unparsable, and deterministic race behavior.
CHANGELOG.md records the cron-runtime lock hardening under ## [Unreleased] in the same commit as the runtime change.
userStories.notes: Completed iteration 1 on 2026-06-16; atomic acquireLock, race tests, changelog committed in branch history

userStories.title: Update the cron-runtime wiki model
userStories.description: As a future agent, I want the cron runtime wiki entry to mention singleton locking so I understand how scheduler duplication is prevented.
acceptanceCriteria: wiki/cron-runtime.md documents acquireLock() as the singleton guard with source-file line references updated for the final code.
The wiki system-relationships section mentions the lock before schedule arming.
wiki/cron-runtime.md includes a short operator recovery note for a wedged/stale lock.
bash evals/probes/wiki-readme-index.sh passes; wiki/README.md is regenerated only if needed.
userStories.notes: Completed iteration 2 on 2026-06-16; cron-runtime wiki lock model and recovery note added
